Marketers in the Arena / Stellar House Mexico
Event Retrospective · April 2026
Event Retrospective · Prepared for Stellar Foundation & Myosin

STELLAR
HOUSE
MEXICO

Dates April 20–22, 2026
Location Mexico City
Format 3-Day Programming

A three-day programming experience that brought together the Stellar ecosystem with the Mexican builder and founder community — spanning an intimate ecosystem dinner, a community-forward day of open programming, and a high-caliber institutional day with major organizations.

Our role was strategic, not operational: we curated content, shaped programming with an education-first lens, and served as a connector between the Stellar Foundation and the local ecosystem.

"Leaving Mexico feeling very bullish on what's being built here, and excited for what's next. Thinking it might be time to bring Marketers in the Arena to CDMX." — Estefania Ochoa

3 Days of Programming Apr 20–22, 2026
2 Workshops Education-first sessions
12 Ecosystem Dinner Guests Intimate, curated table
545 LinkedIn Impressions 330 members reached · 21 reactions
1.2K+ Total Social Views X + LinkedIn combined

WHO WAS IN THE ROOM

April 20 · Ecosystem Dinner · 12 Guests

Attendees represented some of the most active organizations building in the Mexican and LATAM web3 and fintech space. A table of people building in parallel — who benefit from knowing each other.

10 confirmed attendees + Tomer (Stellar Foundation) and Dave (Etherfuse Founder).

EtherfuseStablecoin Infrastructure · LATAM
SykloWeb3 / DeFi
Base LATAMEcosystem
Blockchain ForoCommunity
OpportunitiFintech
+ 7 More Guests← Add from guest list
Day 03 · April 22 Institutional
Day

The Room
Changed.

Institutions · Capital · Senior Decision-Makers

Day three shifted the register significantly. The room changed composition — institutional organizations, serious capital, and senior decision-makers filled the space, reflecting Stellar's positioning as a platform that has moved well beyond its early adopter phase.

The defining moment of the day — and arguably of the week — was listening to Denelle Dixon speak. Her clarity, composure, and genuine optimism about the trajectory of Stellar were reassuring. She spoke from conviction and deep institutional knowledge.

What resonated most was the accessibility of Stellar's executive team. There was no performance of distance or hierarchy — just a team genuinely engaged with the ecosystem they're building. They closed with an executive lineup open for questions from the room. For a LATAM founder deciding where to build, the signal that leadership is present, reachable, and invested is not a small thing.

"There was no performance of distance or hierarchy. Just a team that is genuinely engaged with the ecosystem they're building."
On Denelle Dixon & the Stellar Executive Team · Institutional Day, April 22

KEY TAKEAWAYS

01
Marketing Education
for Founders

Helping builders in the Stellar ecosystem understand how to communicate their products, position themselves, and grow their communities. This was our core mandate across all three days.

02
Ecosystem
Connectivity

Facilitating introductions and relationships that outlast the event itself. The pre-dinner was the clearest expression of this intent — twelve people, a shared meal, conversations that continued through the rest of the week.

03
LATAM Isn't
Emerging

The founders are serious and the appetite for what Stellar enables is genuine. The trust being placed in Stellar — by builders, institutions, and teams choosing to embed themselves in this ecosystem — is not incidental.

OUR PROGRAMMING

2 Speaker Sessions · Founder-Focused Workshops
Session 01 Marketing 101:
For Blockchain Founders Brandon Rodriguez · Etherfuse

Great ideas and even strong products often fail to gain traction in the early stages. Being technically strong is rarely enough. This session broke down a practical go-to-market approach for small teams looking to break through the noise, gain focus, and prioritize scarce resources.

Sharpen Positioning Improve Messaging Distribution Thinking Resource Prioritization

Brandon leads Marketing at Etherfuse, the global leader in tokenizing sovereign debt. Former Paystand, Reach, Visa, Samsung, Intel.

Session 02 Community Is Not
a Channel Victor Hernandez · Plume Network

This session challenged web3's most common assumption — that community is a growth channel. Drawing from building 1M+ member communities at BNB Chain, Bitso, and Plume entirely pre-product, Victor introduced a framework built around Mission, Belonging, and Momentum.

The core argument: most web3 communities fail because they're built on incentives instead of identity. The startups that win treat community not as a channel to broadcast into, but as a culture to build from day one.

Mission Belonging Momentum

8+ years in Web3. Grew Plume Network X to 1M+ followers pre-product. Expanded BNB Chain LATAM community 150% across 6 countries. Built Bitso organic channels from scratch to 30k members.

LOOKING
FORWARD

Stellar House Mexico confirmed what we had suspected: the LATAM ecosystem is not emerging. It's been here. And there's a natural next step.

→
Marketers in the Arena — CDMX The trust built at Stellar House Mexico creates a direct runway for a deeper, more focused event: a curated room where the builders in this ecosystem get real marketing feedback and high-signal connections. June 17th, 2026.
→
Deepening the Ecosystem The relationships seeded at the dinner, the workshops, and the institutional day are the foundation. The next event builds on top of them — not from scratch.
→
LATAM as a Strategic Priority With $60B+ in annual remittances and stablecoin rails being built in real-time by teams like Etherfuse, Mexico City is not a regional outpost. It's a strategic centre.
